spec_match.rationale: The demo accurately shows that fragment navigation is case-sensitive in standards mode, and uses querySelector to verify the exact matching behavior.

interactive.rationale: The user can click the different fragment links and click 'Check current fragment' to see if the anchor was matched under case-sensitive rules.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb correctly highlights the inconsistency of quirks mode vs standards mode matching and Chrome 150's fix.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo itself runs in standards mode and cannot directly demonstrate the quirks mode bug or its fix, only illustrating it through a static comparison matrix.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus entry and MDN links clearly.

design_system.rationale: Adapts to the platform showcase design system, using clear test-cards and distinct color-coded badges for success/failure.

openQuestions.title: Support quirks mode simulation via iframe
openQuestions.detail: Since this page has a DOCTYPE, it only executes in standards mode. An iframe-based quirks mode simulation (loading a document without a DOCTYPE) would allow testing the actual bugs and Chrome 150's fix live in-page, rather than relying only on static tables.

summary: A functional demonstration of case-sensitive fragment navigation, though restricted to standards mode since the main page contains a DOCTYPE declaration.
